Title: Why IST
Content: Space is considered to be the final frontier and world’s best minds take up the most daunting challenges to conquer it. Fazeel Mahmood Khan, PhD The Institute of Space Technology (IST) is a premier institution in Pakistan, renowned for its focus on space and related sciences. Here's why IST stands out: Why Choose IST? 1. Specialized Education: IST offers specialized education in space and related sciences, equipping students to address national and global issues. 2. Research Opportunities: The institute has state-of-the-art research labs, providing students with hands-on experience and opportunities to contribute to cutting-edge research. 3. Accreditation and Recognition: IST is federally chartered, HEC recognized, and PEC accredited, ensuring its degrees are valued globally. 4. Industry Connections: IST has strong industry connections, facilitating internships, placements, and job opportunities for its graduates. 5. Student Life: The institute offers a vibrant student life, with various societies, clubs, and sports activities, fostering personal growth and development. 6. Scholarships and Sponsorships: IST offers merit-based scholarships and sponsorships, making quality education accessible to deserving students. 7. Global Recognition: IST's programs are accredited under Level-II by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC), substantially equivalent to Washington Accord, enabling graduates to work globally. Unique Achievements 1. Satellite Development: IST has successfully developed and launched satellites, including iCUBE1 and ICUBE-Qamar, demonstrating its expertise in space technology. 2. Research and Innovation: The institute is committed to research and innovation, with a strong focus on solving real-world problems. Career Opportunities At the IST, we empower students to pursue their passion for space science and technology. Since 2002, we've been producing professionals who meet the demands of national organizations and contribute to the nation's technological advancements. Thriving Careers Ahead Our graduates enjoy excellent career prospects, both locally and globally. They're employed in leading organizations, including: • Space agencies (SUPARCO, European Space Agencies) • Aviation industry (Airbus, Emirates, Air Blue) • Research institutes (R&D, academia) • Government organizations (PTA, WAPDA, EPA) • Private sector (mobile communication, agriculture, airlines) Diverse Employment Opportunities Our multidisciplinary approach opens doors to various industries, contributing to socioeconomic activities nationwide. Our graduates work in: • Space science and technology • Aerospace engineering • Research and development • Telecommunications • Meteorology • Agriculture and food sector Global Recognition IST graduates are recognized globally, with opportunities to pursue further education at renowned universities worldwide. If you're passionate about space science and technology, IST is the perfect platform to launch your career. With our strong industry connections, research opportunities, and global recognition, you'll be well-equipped to succeed in your chosen field.

Title: Admission FAQ
Content:
Q: What is the application process?
A: The application process involves filling out an online application form, uploading required documents, and paying the application processing fee. Online admission module can be accessed by clicking the link IST Admissions
Q: How many seats are for each program?
A: Engineering Programs: Aerospace Engineering : 100 - Avionics Engineering : 80 – Electrical Engineering : 100 – Mechanical Engineering : 120 – Metallurgy & Materials Engineering : 80 Computing Programs: Computer Science : 100 – Artificial Intelligence : 50 – Data Science : 50 – Software Engineering: 50 – Computer Engineering: 50 Science Programs : Space Science : 50 – Physics: 40 – Math: 50 – RS&GIS: 50 – Chemistry (Nanotechnology): 50 – Biotechnology: 50
Q: What is duration of study for each Program?
A: The study duration of each program is 04 years (08 semesters).
Q: How many times admissions are announced for Engineering, Non-Engineering & Computing Programs in a year?
A: Admissions for BS programs are announced once in a year i.e. in March/ April.
Q: Does an applicant can apply for all engineering, computing & science programs?
A: Yes.
Q: What is the eligibility criteria, merit determination criteria & fee structure for BS programs?
A: Updated information is available and can be accessed by using the below links: https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=general-eligibility&-scholarship= https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=merit-determination-criteria&-scholarship= https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=fee-structure-bs-programs&-scholarship=
Q: Which entry test is required to apply for BS engineering programs?
A: IST doesn’t conduct its own entry test. Please use the below link for details: https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=entry-test&-scholarship=
Q: I have already appeared for entry test but I do not receive result card. What should I do?
A: Please get a print of your result card from website of testing authority and upload with online application form
Q: Which entry test is required to apply for BS computing & non-engineering programs?
Q: Does the institute have affiliation with any university/ institution?
A: IST is a federally chartered, HEC recognized and PEC accredited, degree awarding university therefore it does not require affiliation with any other university/ institution. Please use the below link for details: https://www.hec.gov.pk/english/universities/pages/recognised.aspx
Q: Does Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC) recognize the Institute?
A: IST has been awarded accreditation for its various undergraduate engineering programs at Level-II by Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC), making them fully compliant with the Washington Accord. Engineering graduates obtaining a degree under this category of HEIs are considered at par with world class professionals in the field, and do not require any further evaluation of their degrees for professional jobs or higher education abroad.
Q: Does National Computing Education Accreditation Council (NCEAC) recognize the Institute?
A: All Computing programs offered by the institute are duly approved/ accredited by the National Computing Education Accreditation Council (NCEAC).
Q: What is the medium of teaching/ instructions/ examination/ extracurricular activities at IST?
A: All educational and extracurricular activities such as “Class Lectures, Oral and Written Examinations, Quizzes, Assignments, Seminars, Conferences, Notifications etc” at Institute of Space Technology (IST) Islamabad take place in English Language. Therefore, IST graduates may not face any difficulty in performing job/ pursuing higher education at any institution/ university/ organization around the globe where the medium of communication/ teaching/ instructions/ examination is English.
Q: Does IST have reserved seats for overseas/ dual nationality holder applicants?
A: There are no reserve seats for overseas/ dual nationality holder applicants. All admissions in IST are based on transparent open merit basis.
Q: Does IST have reserved seats for DAE applicants?
A: There are no reserved seats for Diploma of Associate Engineering (DAE) applicants. However, DAE holders can apply on open merit, competing with FSc Pre-Engineering and ICS students.
Q: Does IST allow result awaiting DAE applicants to apply for admission in IST?
A: Result awaiting DAE applicants are not eligible to apply for admission in IST.
Q: Does IST allow pre-medical applicants to apply for admission in BS engineering & science programs?
A: Those pre-medical candidates are allowed to apply for admission in BS engineering & science programs who have already passed additional math papers or appeared for additional math papers and waiting for result announcement. OR by completing 8-week condensed Mathematics course from any university.
Q: Does IST allow pre-medical students to apply for admission in BS computing programs?
A: Yes, pre-medical students can apply for all computing programs.
Q: Does the institute provide hostel facility?
A: Yes, IST offers furnished hostel facilities for both boys and girls within the campus, providing a convenient and secure living environment for students.
Q: What is the lowest merit of last years for BS programs?
A: Lowest merit for each program has remained different every year. Therefore, apply online irrespective of lowest merits of last years.
Q: What are the Sponsorships/ Scholarships are offered by IST for BS students?
A: Details about the Sponsorships/ Schlarships can be accessed by using the below link: https://ist.edu.pk/admission?section=financial-aid-and-scholarships&-scholarship=
Q: Does IST have enough qualified faculty?
A: Yes, IST has enough highly qualified and experienced faculty in the respective areas.
Q: Does IST have proper lab facilities?
A: IST is proud to maintain well-equipped laboratories that fully support our academic programs. Additionally, we have established specialized laboratories to equip students with technical proficiency and expertise in practical equipment handling, thereby ensuring their readiness for real-world challenges
Q: Does IST have a library?
A: Yes, IST has a resourceful library with sufficient current editions of books, reference material to support the programs, which is constantly updated. Library is also subscribing more than 14 periodicals and online professional publications.
Q: What are the prospects of higher education?
A: The IST programs are accredited nationally and internationally and the student shall have no difficulty in getting admissions abroad.
Q: What is the System of Education at IST?
A: IST follows the semester system. Each program is of eight semesters and each semester is of eighteen weeks duration. There will be two semesters in each year.
Q: Is it mandatory for students of O-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification to obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC before the closing date to apply for admission in IST?
A: Yes, it is a mandatory requirement for students of O-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification to obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC and upload with online application form before the closing date to apply for admission in IST.
Q: Is it mandatory for students of A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification to obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC before the closing date of application to apply for admission in IST?
A: Yes, it is mandatory requirement if an applicant have passed/ completed A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification. In other case, such students are required to apply for equivalence certificate immediately after the completion/ announcement of results of A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification from concerned boards.
Q: How students can apply for equivalence certificate in current environment?
A: IBCC has introduced an online system to apply for equivalence certificate. Email address/ contact numbers are also mentioned on IBCC website to obtain desired/ relevant information. Please visit IBCC website https://www.ibcc.edu.pk and apply online for equivalence certificates of O-Level/ A-Level/ equivalent foreign qualification.
Q: How much time is given to new BS students for the submission of final FSC result card/ equivalent certificate if it is not available at the time of submission of online admission form?
A: It is mandatory for all BS students to submit the final FSC result card/ IBCC equivalent certificate on registration day. In case of delay in the announcement of results from concerned board, students are required to submit the final FSC result card/ IBCC equivalent certificate within two weeks after the announcements of results from concerned boards otherwise their admission will be cancelled.
Q: What if student of FSC/ A-Level/ equivalent level foreign qualification comes to know after the announcement of result that he/ she could not meet the eligibility requirements of IST?
A: Admission of such students will be cancelled. It is the responsibility of such students to immediately inform Admissions Office so that their admission can be cancelled and full fee can be refunded. Disciplinary action can be initiated in case of delay or hiding the result for longer time from IST. In such case, fee will be refunded as per IST fee refund policy.
Q: Is it mandatory to deposit application processing fee?
A: Yes, it is mandatory to deposit application processing fee in any online branch of HBL otherwise your application will not be processed/considered for admission in IST. Application processing fee is non-refundable in any case.
Q: How it will be confirmed that whether my application processing fee has been received at IST? Or I have to send copy of Paid Chillan form to IST.
A: Nothing is required to send IST. Candidates are required to provide payment details and upload challan forms/ transaction slip with online admission form. Your fee status will be automatically updated on your application form within next 4 to 5 working days after deposit of application processing fee in any online branch of HBL. You can log-in and check the status.
Q: Can I deposit application processing fee through online bank transfer?
A: It is strongly recommended to deposit your application processing fee through bank challan available at online admission form.
Q: What should I do if I have paid application processing but my fee status is not updated on online application form?
A: You are required to email scan copy of paid challan form at “admissions@ist.edu.pk” before the closing date to apply for admission in IST.
Q: Is it mandatory to attach the documents with online application form?
A: Yes, it is mandatory to attach the desired documents with your online application form otherwise your application will not be processed/considered for admission in IST.
Q: I have lost one of the documents which are required to attach with online application form. What should I do?
A: Apply for duplicate copy from concerned board. Meanwhile you can download your result card from website of concerned board and attached with your online application form.
Q: I have lost my FSC (Part-1) result card but I have FSC (part-1 + part 2 combined) final result card. What should I do?
A: Please upload FSC final result card with your online application form.
Q: What I have to mention in admission form if my guardian don’t have (National Tax Number) NTN?
A: Mention NTN number of your guardian, if available, otherwise leave that cell empty.
Q: What does it mean by Guardian?
A: A guardian is a person who is looking after you in terms of taking your care, paying your educational expenses etc.
Q: My aggregate score in 1st year is less than 60%. Can I apply for admission in IST?
A: If you are sure that you will be able to improve your score after FSC, as per eligibility criteria mentioned on IST website for respective program, then you may apply for admission in IST based on your score in FSC (Part-1).
Q: I have completed FSC/ ICS. Can I mention the overall score of FSC or my merit will be determined on the basis of overall FSC/ ICS marks?
A: You are required to mention the score of FSC/ ICS (Part-1) where required because as per merit determination criteria, merit will be determined based on the marks obtained in FSC/ ICS (Part-1).
Q: When my application will be processed?
A: If you have deposited application processing fee then please wait. Your application will be processed soon.
Q: When and where merit lists are displayed?
A: Merit lists are posted on IST website within one week after the closing date to apply for admission in IST.
Q: Is it required to send hard copy of online application form/ Challan Form or documents to IST?
A: You are required to upload desired documents with your online admission form. Do not send application form or any other document to IST.
Q: What is the last date to deposit application processing fee?
A: Last date to deposit application processing fee is same as closing date to apply for admission in IST.
Q: What have to mention by O-Level or A-Level students on online application form where Reference Number is required?
A: Reference Number is mentioned just below the line where obtained marks by the student are mentioned on equivalence certificate.
Q: What have to mention by O-Level or A-Level students on online application form where Serial Number is required?
A: Serial Number is given on top right corner of equivalence certificate.
Q: What have to mention by FSC students on online application form where Registration Number is required?
A: FSC students are required to mention registration number issued by concerned board.
Q: Can I edit information in my online application form after submission?
A: Yes, you can edit/update your online application form up to closing date to apply for admission in IST by using your log-in details. In the same way, you can also upload or replace existing documents which are attached with your online application form. When you will upload new document, the previous one will be deleted automatically.
Q: Can I access my online admission form on mobile?
A: Yes, you can access/ edit your online admission form through mobile. However, for better experience, please use desktop computer to access/ edit the information in your online admission form.
Q: I have error in my email address or CNIC number. How I can rectify it?
A: You are required to send an email at “admissions@ist.edu.pk” highlighting the problem which you are facing while creating login or accessing your online application form.
Q: I have completed my FSc (pre-medical) and appearing/appeared for additional math paper. Can I apply for admission to Engineering programs?
A: Yes, you can apply for admission to Engineering programs in IST based on your score in FSc pre-medical (part-1).
Note:
The university reserves the right to make any corrections or changes, as and when required, without prior notice.
Admission FAQ

Title: Foreign Applicant
Content:
--- Al Khwarizmi Scholarships for Foreign Students ---
Scholarships are available for Foreign Students to study in Bachelor/ Master/ Doctoral Degree Programs offered by IST. Duration of Scholarship: For BS (Bachelor’s) Students: Upto 04 years For MS (Master’s) Students: Upto 2.5 years For PhD (Doctoral) Students: Upto 04 years The scholarship program covers funds for the following expenses: University tuition fee as per actual Stipend @ PKR 15000, 20000 & 35000 per month for students admitted in BS, MS & PhD programs respectively Stipend @ PKR 50000 per month for scientist/ post doc fellow One time return air ticket Mandatory academic & non-academic charges like admission fee, endowment fund, library development fund, service charges, safe/ smart campus charges, hostel charges etc. are not covered.
--- Admission of Foreign Applicants in IST ---
--- Admission Pre-requisites ---
Candidates willing to apply for admission in BS, MS/ PhD programs offered by IST must: meet the eligibility requirements ( BS , MS , PhD ) for respective program as mentioned on IST website be able/ willing to pay the semester dues/ allied charges as per fee structure ( BS , MS, PhD ) given on IST website for foreign Students. All fees and other charges are subject to revision from time to time be proficient in English as all educational and extracurricular activities such as "Class Lectures, Oral and Written Examinations, Quizzes, Assignments, Seminars, Conferences, Notifications etc" at Institute of Space Technology (IST) Islamabad take place in English Language obtain equivalence certificate from IBCC / HEC , Pakistan (keeping in view the eligibility requirements to apply for respective program) before the closing date of application to apply for admission in IST. In case any document proves to be false, fake, or fabricated at a later stage, a provisionally admitted student shall be liable to expulsion from the University at any stage or to any other disciplinary or legal action the University may deem necessary obtain No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their Ministry of Foreign Affairs or their Embassy in Pakistan prepare personal statement, in English, should include self-introduction, study and work experience, reasons for application and study plan/ research proposal obtain two letters of recommendation from full professors or associate professors, in English. (Professors’ signature, contact phone number and email address must be put on the letter) be willing to meet/ abide all requirements/ rules & regulations issued by the IST/ HEC/ Concerned Government Organizations/ Departments from time to time possess adequate mental and physical health to continue his/ her course of studies
--- Procedure ---
Admissions will be announced by IST in national newspapers/ on IST website Foreign applicants meeting the pre-requisites will be required to apply online at IST’s website www.ist.edu.pk Foreign applicants will be required to complete online application form in all respects including the deposit/ online transfer of application processing fee before the last date to apply for admission in IST for further process Merit of foreign applicants will be determined according the merit determination criteria mentioned on IST website Merit lists of provisionally selected BS and MS candidates shall be displayed on IST website whereas, provisionally selected PhD candidates will be intimated through email Provisionally selected BS, MS & PhD candidates will deposit admission dues as per details shared by IST to secure their seat Cases of selected foreign applicants for provisional admission in IST will be referred to Higher Education Commission (HEC) and relevant Government department(s) along with their passport copy, photographs, admission letter, NoC of their Embassy/ Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Student’s Information Sheet for Issuance of No Objection Certificate (NoC)/ final approval as per the policy in vogue Selected foreign applicants for provisional admission in IST will be required to approach the Pakistani mission abroad for the visa and submits the following documents: Application Form (Student Visa Form) Photocopy of Passport Photograph Educational Documents Admission Letter of University No Objection Certificate (NOC) issued by the Home Country to Study in Pakistan Any other document if required by Pakistan Embassy at respective country Joining instructions will be issued to the provisionally selected foreign applicants after: The completion of all admission requirements of IST Receiving the intimation/ NOC from HEC/ Concerned Government Organizations Successfully joined foreign applicants/ students will be required to meet/ abide all requirements/ rules & regulations issued by the IST/ HEC/ Concerned Government Organizations/ Departments from time to time IST reserves the right to make amendments in the policy regarding the admission of foreign students at any time without notice/ cancel or refuse admission to any foreign applicant at any time/ stage without assigning any reason
--- Contact Details ---
Incharge Admissions Focal Person (International Student Office) Email: admissions@ist.edu.pk Contact: +92-51-9075100

Title: Financial Aid & Scholarships
Content:
--- Sponsorship for BS Students ---
Several elite National Research & Development (R&D) organizations provide sponsorships to the deserving BS students of selected programs after test/interview. The sponsorship covers tuition fee and a guaranteed job after graduation, provided all requirements of the sponsoring organization are fulfilled by the candidate.
--- Merit Scholarships for BS Students ---
IST offers merit scholarships to three students (excluding sponsored students) in each discipline as per the existing policy. Scholarships are granted on the basis of admissions merit and semester results. Below mentioned criteria shall be followed for the award of merit scholarships to BS students of each batch of each undergraduate program offered by IST:-
Table 1:
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: Ser. | col_1: Scholarship Type | col_2: Merit Position | col_3: Tuition Fee Waiver | col_4: Minimum Criteria
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 01 | col_1: IST-1 | col_2: 1st | col_3: 100 % | col_4: Marks in HSSC/ Eq. > 85%
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 02 | col_1: IST-2 | col_2: 2nd | col_3: 50 % | col_4: Marks in HSSC/ Eq. > 85%
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 03 | col_1: IST-3 | col_2: 3rd | col_3: 25 % | col_4: Marks in HSSC/ Eq. > 85%
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: FOR SUBSEQUENT SEMESTERS:
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: Ser. | col_1: Scholarship Type | col_2: Merit Position | col_3: Tuition Fee Waiver | col_4: Minimum Criteria
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 01 | col_1: IST-1 | col_2: 1st | col_3: 100 % | col_4: SGPA  > 3.50
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 02 | col_1: IST-2 | col_2: 2nd | col_3: 50 % | col_4: SGPA  > 3.50
FOR_FIRST_SEMESTER: 03 | col_1: IST-3 | col_2: 3rd | col_3: 25 % | col_4: SGPA  > 3.50
GENERAL CONDITIONS: Full time students with full load of studies in a regular semester will qualify for the award of Merit Scholarship. Merit/ position for the scholarships will be based on SGPA. However, both SGPA and CGPA will be considered to ascertain the eligibility of the student for Scholarship. Students getting any type of scholarships/ sponsored by any organization are not eligible for merit scholarship. Student will be awarded only one scholarship (merit or need based) from IST’s own funds (whichever is higher) at a time. In case of two or more students securing the same merit position (Admissions’ Merit Position/ same SGPA and CGPA), allocated amounts of respective scholarships for these positions shall be equally distributed among them. If students ‘A’ & ‘B’ jointly declared at 1st position, then the student ‘C’ would be at 3rd position and so on. No scholarship shall be awarded if there is an ‘I’ Grade in preceding semester. No scholarship shall be awarded if there is a ‘W’ Grade in preceding semester. Student should take full load of credit hours in that preceding semester. In case, a student takes any additional/ repeat course(s), his/ her merit position will be calculated on the basis of normal load of credit hours in that semester. In case of semester freeze, merit scholarship would only be awarded after re-joining of the student. Transferred students shall not be eligible for the award of merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they studied with the students of previous department/ program. However, such students shall be reconsidered for merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they will study with the students of their new department/ program. In situations where existing rules & regulations are found silent/unavailable, the decision made by IST will be the final which can’t be challenged anywhere. The contents as stated in merit scholarship policy are expression of intent only. The institute reserves the right to discontinue any portion or make amendments in merit scholarship policy/ rules & regulations at any time without notice.
--- Merit Scholarships for MS Students ---
IST offers merit scholarships to two students (excluding sponsored students) in each discipline as per existing policy. Scholarships are granted on the basis of semester results. Below mentioned criteria shall be followed for the award of merit scholarships to students of each batch of each MS program offered by IST:-
Table 1:
Ser: 1 | Scholarship_Type: IST-1 | Merit_Position: 1st | Tuition_Fee_Waiver: 100% | Minimum_Criteria: SGPA and CGPA > 3.75
Ser: 2 | Scholarship_Type: IST-2 | Merit_Position: 2nd | Tuition_Fee_Waiver: 50% | Minimum_Criteria: SGPA and CGPA > 3.50
GENERAL CONDITIONS: Full time students with full load of studies in a regular semester will qualify for the award of Merit Scholarship. Merit/ position for the scholarships will be based on SGPA. However, both SGPA and CGPA will be considered to ascertain the eligibility of the student for Scholarship. Students getting any type of scholarships/ sponsored by any organization are not eligible for merit scholarship. Student will be awarded only one scholarship (merit or need based) from IST’s own funds (whichever is higher) at a time. In case of two or more students securing the same merit position (Admissions’ Merit Position/ same SGPA and CGPA), allocated amounts of respective scholarships for these positions shall be equally distributed among them. If students ‘A’ & ‘B’ jointly declared at 1st position, then the student ‘C’ would be at 3rd position and so on. No scholarship shall be awarded if there is an ‘I’ Grade in preceding semester. No scholarship shall be awarded if there is a ‘W’ Grade in preceding semester. Student should take full load i.e. minimum 09 credit hours in each 1st & 2nd semester and minimum 06 credit hours in each 3rd & 4th semester. In case, a student takes any additional/ repeat course(s), his/ her merit position will be calculated on the basis of normal load of credit hours in that semester. In case of semester freeze, merit scholarship would only be awarded after re-joining of the student. Transferred students shall not be eligible for the award of merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they studied with the students of previous department/ program. However, such students shall be reconsidered for merit scholarship on the basis of results of courses which they will study with the students of their new department/ program. In situations where existing rules & regulations are found silent/unavailable, the decision made by IST will be the final which can’t be challenged anywhere. The contents as stated in merit scholarship policy are expression of intent only. The institute reserves the right to discontinue any portion or make amendments in merit scholarship policy/ rules & regulations at any time without notice.
--- EHSAAS Scholarships for BS Students ---
Higher Education Commission (HEC) with the support of Government of Pakistan has launched “EHSAAS Undergraduate Scholarship Project” for the qualified students from low income families to complete undergraduate studies (Four-Five-year Bachelor programs) from public sector Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) of Pakistan, Azad Jammu & Kashmir, and Gilgit Baltistan. The principle objective of the EHSAAS Undergraduate Project is to provide assistance to the maximum number of students who have secured admission to the universities in various disciplines but lack of financial means to continue their studies. The needy students admitted in IST are informed to apply for EHSAAS Scholarship as and when announced by HEC.
--- HEC Need Based Scholarships for BS Students ---
Pakistan has no shortage of talented youth, but in several instances, lack of resources prevents them from realizing their potentials. This becomes more problematic when it comes to accessing higher education. The Government of Pakistan acknowledges these constraints and has tasked Higher Education Commission to facilitate the needy and deserving students through Need Based Scholarships. Scholarships are announced for financially disadvantaged students of as many as 94 public sector universities and their campuses. The needy students admitted in IST are informed to apply for HEC Need Based Scholarship after the completion of admission process subject to availability of funds/seats.
--- The Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF) ---
The Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF) under guidance of the Board of Directors is providing opportunities to the talented but needy students to fulfill their dreams and aspirations and at the same time to become partner in the development and welfare of Pakistan. The PEEF, since its inception in 2009 has awarded more than 374,000 scholarships to students of Punjab and other provinces which are currently pursuing education through public and private sector institutions. Needy students, admitted in IST, can also apply for this scholarship. The details are available at https://www.peef.org.pk/Scholarships_Main.html
--- Loans ---
Interest free loans are also provided to IST students by National Bank of Pakistan (NBP). The process to apply for loan and other details are available at https://www.nbp.com.pk/studentloan/
Financial Aid & Scholarships

--- Flow Chart ---
--- Steps of Enrollment in BS Programs offered by IST ---
Step 1 - Admissions Announcement on IST Website/ National Newspapers Undergraduate (BS) admissions are announced on IST Website/ National Newspapers once a year i.e. in March/April. Step 2 - Completion of Online Admission Form Candidates interested to apply for admission in IST are required to visit www.ist.edu.pk or https://eportal.ist.edu.pk/ to create an online account, read & understand the eligibility & merit determination criteria, fee structure, fee refund policy and other related information. Subsequently, eligible candidates willing to apply for admission in IST are required to complete online admission form by providing desired information and uploading the scan copies of required documents with online admission form. Step 3 - Deposit of Application Processing Fee After creating an online account, applicants are required to download the challan form available at online admission form to deposit of application processing fee (non-refundable) in any online branch of Meezan Bank/HBL (within due date). It is a mandatory requirement. An email is sent for the confirmation of receipt of application processing fee and in parallel; fee receipt status is also updated at online admission form. In case if the application processing fee status is not updated at online admission within one week after the deposit of application processing fee then such applicants are required to contact Admissions Office with proof through email. Step 4 - Merit Lists/ Admissions Status Applicants are required to check their admission status on IST website by using the login details. An email/ SMS is also sent to provisionally selected applicants. However, applicants are strongly advised to keep visiting the IST website for updated information. Step 5 – Acceptance of Admission Provisionally selected applicants in respective programs are required to download the admission package by accessing the online admission system preferably through IST website. Admission package contain the offer letter, fee challan form/s, an affidavit (write-up), fee refund policy and other documents required to be submitted for registration in IST. Step 6 – Reservation of Seat to avail Hostel/ Transport Facility (optional) After the acceptance of admission offer, on the same page of online admission system, candidates are required to reserve hostel /transport seat ( if they want to avail services of IST otherwise skip this section ). Transport facility is provided only to the students of Rawalpindi & Islamabad from the designated points. IST have within campus separate hostel facility for male and female students with limited capacity therefore interested applicants can avail hostel facility subject to availability of seats. Step 7 – Deposit of Fee Candidates fulfilling the admissions requirements and willing to join IST are required to deposit the fee in any online branch of Meezan Bank/HBL within due date mentioned on challan forms. An email/ SMS is also sent for the confirmation of receipt of fee. It is highlighted that fully automated admission system of IST does not allow a late fee option. Therefore, nonpayment of dues within due date will make you in-eligible for admission in IST and consideration in subsequent merit lists and no request is entertained in this regard. Step 8 – Welcome to IST on Registration Day Provisionally admitted students are required to prepare the documents according to Offer Letter for the submission on Registration Day.

Title: Entry Test
Content:
--- Entry Test for Engineering Programs – Fall 2026 Intake ---
IST doesn’t conduct its own entry test. The results of following entry tests are acceptable to apply for admission in undergraduate programs offered by IST. Applicants can appear in any one of the below mentioned tests and can use the higher score to apply for admission in IST. Please note that an applicant must have minimum 33% obtained marks in entry test to apply for admission in IST: NAT which is conducted by NTS in every month HAT which is conducted by ETC-HEC NET which is conducted by NUST Islamabad ECAT which is conducted by UET Lahore ETEA which is conducted by UET Peshawar Entry Test for Engineering Programs conducted by MUET Jamshoro Entry Test for Engineering Programs conducted by NED Karachi ACT which is conducted by ACT-USA Further details related to acceptable entry tests dates & categories are as under: NAT Acceptable Test Dates & Categories The test is conducted by NTS in all major cities of Pakistan in every month. Interested students are required to visit NTS website i.e. https://www.nts.org.pk/new/NAT.php#vd or contact NTS to get desired information related to the registration procedure, test dates and centers etc. Acceptable Dates: The results of the NAT conducted by NTS from July 01, 2025 to July 31, 2026 are acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST. Acceptable Categories: NAT-1E and NAT-1CS, both are acceptable to apply for admission in BS Engineering Programs HAT Acceptable Test Dates & Categories HEC has established Education Testing Commission (ETC) to hold Higher Education Aptitude Test (HAT) for UG admissions. Acceptable Dates: The results of the HAT-UG tests announced/ available from July 01, 2025 to July 31, 2026 are acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST. Acceptable Categories: HAT–UG–E and HAT–UG–CS, both are acceptable to apply for admission in BS Engineering Programs NET Acceptable Test Dates & Categories The result of NUST Entry Test (NET) announced/ available from December, 2025 to July 31, 2026 are acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST. Acceptable Categories: NET (Engineering Category), NET (Computer Science) both are acceptable to apply for admission in all BS Engineering Programs ECAT Acceptable Test Date & Category The result of ECAT conducted by University of Engineering & Technology (UET) Lahore for Fall 2026 intake in Engineering Programs announced/ available before last date of admissions announced by IST will be acceptable to apply for admission in all BS Engineering Programs offered by IST. ETEA Acceptable Test Date & Category The result of ETEA conducted by University of Engineering & Technology (UET) Peshawar for Fall 2026 intake in Engineering Programs announced/ available before last date of admissions announced by IST will be acceptable to apply for admission in all BS Engineering Programs offered by IST. MUET Jamshoro Acceptable Test Date & Category The result of Entry Test conducted by Mehran University of Engineering & Technology (MUET), Jamshoro for Fall 2026 intake in Engineering Programs announced/ available before last date of admissions announced by IST will be acceptable to apply for admission in all BS Engineering Programs offered by IST. NED UET Acceptable Test Date & Category The result of Entry Test conducted by NED University of Engineering & Technology, Karachi for Fall 2026 intake in Engineering Programs announced/ available before last date of admissions announced by IST will be acceptable to apply for admission in all BS Engineering Programs offered by IST. ACT Acceptable Test Dates The composite score of ACT conducted from July 01, 2025 to July 31, 2026 is acceptable to apply for admission in BS programs offered by IST. Candidates are required to enter their ACT Composite Score in their online application form and upload the score card available in “MyACT account” otherwise their application will not be processed.

--- Steps of Enrollment in MS & PhD Programs offered by IST ---
Step 1 - Admissions Announcement on IST Website/ National Newspapers Graduate (MS & PhD) admissions are announced on IST Website/ National Newspapers twice a year i.e. in April/ May & November/ December. Step 2 - Completion of Online Admission Form Candidates interested to apply for admission in IST are required to visit www.ist.edu.pk or https://eportal.ist.edu.pk/ to create an online account, read & understand the eligibility & merit determination criteria, fee structure, fee refund policy and other related information. Subsequently, eligible candidates willing to apply for admission in IST are required to complete online admission form by providing desired information and uploading the scan copies of required documents with online admission form. Step 3 - Deposit of Application Processing Fee After creating an online account, applicants are required to download the challan form available at online admission form to deposit of application processing fee (non-refundable) in any online branch of HBL (within due date). It is a mandatory requirement. An email is sent for the confirmation of receipt of application processing fee and in parallel; fee receipt status is also updated at online admission form. In case if the application processing fee status is not updated at online admission within one week after the deposit of application processing fee then such applicants are required to contact Admissions Office with proof through email. Step 4 - Merit Lists/ Admissions Status MS Applicants are required to check their admission status on IST website by using the login details. An email/ SMS is also sent to provisionally selected applicants. However, applicants are strongly advised to keep visiting the IST website for updated information. PhD applicants recommended by the concerned academic department are intimated by Admissions Office through email/ telephone call. Step 5 – Acceptance of Admission Provisionally selected applicants in respective programs are required to download the admission package by accessing the online admission system preferably through IST website. Admission package contain the offer letter, fee challan form/s, an affidavit (write-up), fee refund policy and other documents required to be submitted for registration in IST. Step 6 – Reservation of Seat to avail Hostel/ Transport Facility (optional) After the acceptance of admission offer, on the same page of online admission system, candidates are required to reserve hostel /transport seat ( if they want to avail services of IST otherwise skip this section ). Transport facility is provided only to the students of Rawalpindi & Islamabad from the designated points. IST have within campus separate hostel facility for male and female students with limited capacity therefore interested applicants can avail hostel facility subject to availability of seats. Step 7 – Deposit of Fee Candidates fulfilling the admissions requirements and willing to join IST are required to deposit the fee in any online branch of HBL within due date mentioned on challan forms. An email/ SMS is also sent for the confirmation of receipt of fee. It is highlighted that fully automated admission system of IST does not allow a late fee option. Therefore, nonpayment of dues within due date will make you in-eligible for admission in IST. Step 8 – Welcome to IST on Registration Day Provisionally admitted students are required to prepare the documents according to Offer Letter for the submission on Registration Day.

Title: Merit Determination Criteria - PhD

Title: Fee Refund Policy
Content:
The date of request for fee refund claims will be considered from the date of receipt of application through email at refund@ist.edu.pk To apply for refund, an applicant is required to email refund request at refund@ist.edu.pk along-with the reason of leaving IST, scan copy of paid challan form/s & copy of CNIC of Guardian Application Processing Fee and Admission Fee are non-refundable. From Registration, all students are required to complete NDC to process the refund of dues (if any) as per fee refund policy Tuition Fee, Sports Fee, Library Fee and Laboratory Fee, service charges, Safe/Smart Campus Charges, Endowment Fund, Library Development Fund are refundable within 15 days of convene of classes on a pro rata basis as given below. No refund shall be admissible after 15 days of convene of classes either one joins IST/avail facilities or not.
Table 1:
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Compulsory_Fees: Up to 7th day of convene of classes | age_of_Refund: 100%
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Compulsory_Fees: From 8th – 15th day of convene of classes | age_of_Refund: 50%
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Compulsory_Fees: From 16th day of convene of classes | age_of_Refund: 0%
If admission is offered after commencement of classes, date of commencement of classes will be considered as mentioned in offer letter. Adjustment of student liability from 2nd Semester onward will be based on class attendance & facility will be charged as per actual. The application for the adjustment of student’s liability will only be entertained on the verification by concerned HoD and approval of Registrar. In case of Admission cancellation in 1st semester cheque of fee refund will be issued in the name of student’s father/Guardian. 100% dues will be refunded in case where student could not attain requisite marks/grade for admission as prescribed by the institute. This policy is only applicable on fresh admissions. However Optional Charges will be deducted as per actual usage of facilities based on 4.5 Month/Semester Basis. In case of semester freeze, fee once deposited will be adjusted in next semester as per freeze policy after re-joining the student. If student wants to leave the institute after freezing his semester, then dues will be refunded according to refund policy present at the time of freezing the semester. Transport Charges and Hostel Charges (Dormitory Charges, Shuttle Service Charges, Washing Charges) are refundable within 30 days of registration on a pro rata basis as given below. No refund shall be admissible after 30 days of registration either one joins IST/avails facilities or not. However, refund will be calculated based on the date of application or date of leaving the facilities whichever is later.
Table 1:
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Optional_Dues: Up to 7th day of Registration | age_of_Refund: 90%
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Optional_Dues: From 8th to 15th day of Registration | age_of_Refund: 75%
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Optional_Dues: From 16th to 21st day of Registration | age_of_Refund: 50%
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Optional_Dues: From 22nd to 30th day of Registration | age_of_Refund: 25%
Timeline_for_Refund_of_Optional_Dues: From 31st day of Registration | age_of_Refund: 0%
In case of refund of optional dues (Hostel + Transport) before registration day, 100% dues will be refunded. Conversion of Hostel Facility into Transport Facility (subject to availability) within two months will be adjusted as per actual use of facility (4.5 Months basis). After two months, transport facility will be provided free of cost. However, conversion of Transport Facility into Hostel Facility (subject to availability) requires the deposit of deficient amount (Hostel Charges Less Transport Charges). During continual enrollment, a student can avail monthly hostel facility maximum up to two months. Otherwise S/he will have to pay full semester charges.
--- Registration ---
Before the commencement of classes of each semester, all active students are registered for courses offered by respective department. A student shall not be considered to have been registered for the semester unless all previous dues have been paid. Hostel accommodation and transport facilities shall only be provided to students after registration. Every student must update his/her personal information shown in student portal at the start of every semester.
--- Fine for Late Payment ---
The following fine will be levied for payment of fee after due date: For first fifteen days after the due date, 5% of the total payable amount After fifteen days and up to one month after due date, 10% of the total payable amount Students will only be allowed to appear in final exam after deposit of outstanding dues along with fine
--- Mode of Payment ---
Payment of fees can be made through online Transfer/Bank Draft/Pay order against the fee challan issued at any online branch of HBL (Nationwide). Note : Cheques and Cash are not acceptable.

Title: IST Feedback System
Content:
IST has established a proper feedback system for assessing requirements of its stakeholders including student, faculty, and industry, statutory and regulatory bodies. The Quality Enhancement Cell is responsible to ensure that these requirements are gathered and acquired in a reliable manner and properly addressed at the departmental as well as the Vice Chancellor Quality Assurance Committee. IST Feedback system is based on the following important characteristics: IST monitors, reviews and benchmarks teaching and learning processes continuously and brings improvement in learning instructions, teaching, curricula and institutional infrastructure. IST monitors the effectiveness of programs to evaluate the extent to which the intended learning outcomes are being attained by students, and to ensure that appropriate action is taken to remedy any identified shortcomings. IST students are provided full opportunity to raise, individually or collectively, matters of proper concern to them without fear of disadvantage and in the knowledge that privacy and confidentiality will be respected. The mechanisms exist to enable open and constructive feedback to be provided by research students and their supervisors on the learning experience and support infrastructure. The mechanisms are in place to ensure that any conclusions reached by Strategic Quality Management committee are taken into account in considering matters relating to learning, teaching, and assessment. The use of student feedback questionnaires covers all lecture courses, and, wherever appropriate, other course elements. The arrangements made for the analysis of the results of student feedback questionnaires; how and to who are they reported. Subject areas also ensure that there are mechanisms in place to ensure appropriate action in response to any significant issues which arise. IST ensures that where a complaint or appeal is upheld appropriate remedial action is implemented IST has in place effective arrangements for the regular monitoring, evaluation and review of complaints and appeals. IST ensures that information about all complaints and appeals policies and procedures is available in accessible formats and communicated to students.
--- Following are the components of IST Feedback system ---
Document: 1. Faculty Course Review Report (2014-05-15)
Document: 2. Alumni Survey (2012-09-11)
Document: 3. Faculty Survey (2012-09-11)
Document: 4. Faculty Resume Format (2012-09-11)
Document: 5. Employer Survey (2012-09-11)
Document: 6. Graduating Student Survey (2012-09-11)
Document: 7. Research Student Progress Report (2012-09-11)

Title: About Aerospace Engineering
Content:
--- Program Mission Statement ---
The mission of aerospace engineering program is to prepare technically strong engineers for wide ranging careers such that they can contribute effectively towards the nation, society, and aerospace industry through innovation, research, entrepreneurship, leadership, technology and healthy life-long learning attitude.
--- Aerospace Engineering ---
Aerospace is the human effort in science, engineering and business to fly in the atmosphere of Earth (aeronautics) and surrounding space (astronautics). Thus it is a field where state-of-the-art technologies are applied every day. It is an exciting profession with outstanding career opportunities in which physical sciences, mathematics and computers are combined in the design of air and space vehicle systems and components to achieve high performance with limited size and weight. This requires aerospace engineers to constantly develop and apply the most advanced technologies in research, design, manufacturing, operation, or maintenance of an Aerospace Vehicle. Aerospace technology has grown out of the problems of design, construction, and operation of vehicles that maneuver above the Earth’s surface (vehicles ranging from ground-effect machines and helicopters to aircraft and spacecraft). Design of such vehicles has always been challenging, not only because of the requirement that they operate in a hostile environment, but also the high premium placed on light weight, high efficiency and great reliability. These requirements apply not only to future spacecraft and high performance transport aircraft, but also to the next generation of ground transportation, such as high-speed trains, over-water transportation, and automated motor vehicles.
--- Aerospace Engineering Program ---
Aerospace engineering degree program provides graduates with the technical foundation they need to enter the field of Aeronautics & Astronautics. It will prepare them to apply engineering principles, processes and practices to evaluate, analyze, design and develop aerospace systems, and their maintenance. Focused on the field requirements for airborne/spaceborne platforms, the four-year program encompasses the study of aerodynamics, propulsion, aerospace structures, flight dynamics, navigation guidance and control with application of knowledge during aerospace vehicle design. The program imparts knowledge of basic engineering technology as well as practical knowhow for readiness of engineers to cope up the challenges being faced by organizations in Aerospace industry.
--- Future in Aerospace ---
The field of aerospace technology offers a wide range of employment opportunities to those with the proper educational background. An aerospace team is made up of engineers, scientists, and technicians. Positions are available through the private sector as well as within the government sector. Examples of major engineering roles in the aerospace industry include: Analysis Design Materials and Processes Systems Engineering Software Development Manufacturing Flight Research Space Explorations
About Aerospace Engineering

Title: Program Educational Objectives
Content:
--- Under Graduate Aerospace Engineering Program ---

PEO-03: Demonstrate effective communication skills to reflect purpose clarity in complex situations, either individually or as a part of a team. PEO-04: Practice professional management skills and an aptitude for lifelong learning with an aim to maintain balance of life.
--- Program Educational Objectives (PEOs) Graduate Aerospace Engineering Program ---
PEO-1: Produce Aerospace Engineers with a strong practical and theoretical exposure in the relevant disciplines, who are able to contribute to society through innovation, enterprise and leadership PEO-2: Nurture engineer with a global outlook and to provide technological leadership through necessary technological tools PEO-3: Produce engineers with teamwork, communication and interpersonal skills PEO-4: Enable them to be productive members of interdisciplinary engineering teams and are further capable to adopt to changing environments of engineering, technology and society PEO-5: Produce engineers with high moral and ethical values PEO-6: Inculcate critical thinking among students and develop initiatives and innovative ideas

Program Educational Objectives

Title: Program Learning Outcomes
Content:
The Department of Aeronautics and Astronautics has developed Program Learning Outcomes (PLOs) that are supported by our defined Program Educational Objectives. These Program Learning Outcomes relate to the aptitude, awareness and performance that the students acquire with the progression of the program. The following are the PLOs for Aerospace Engineering Program: Engineering Knowledge: An ability to apply knowledge of mathematics, science, engineering fundamentals and an engineering specialization to the solution of complex engineering problems Engineering Problem Analysis: An ability to identify, formulate, research literature and analyze complex engineering problems reaching substantiated conclusions using first principles of mathematics, natural sciences and engineering sciences Design or Development of Solutions: An ability to design solutions for complex engineering problems and design systems, components or processes that meet specified needs with appropriate consideration for public health and safety, cultural, societal, and environmental considerations Investigation: An ability to conduct investigation into complex problems using research based knowledge and research methods including design of experiments, analysis and interpretation of data, and synthesis of information to provide valid conclusions Modern Tool Usage: An ability to create, select and apply appropriate techniques, resources, and modern engineering and IT tools, including prediction and modelling, to complex engineering activities, with an understanding of the limitations. Engineer and Society: An ability to apply reasoning informed by contextual knowledge to assess societal, health, safety, legal and cultural issues and the consequent responsibilities relevant to the professional engineering practices Environment and Sustainability: An ability to understand the impact of professional engineering solutions in societal and environmental contexts and demonstrate knowledge of and need for sustainable development Ethics: An ability to apply ethical principles and commit to professional ethics and responsibilities and norms of engineering practices Individual and Team Work: An ability to function effectively as an individual, and as a member or leader in diverse teams and in multi-disciplinary settings Communication: An ability to communicate effectively on complex engineering activities with the engineering community and with society at large, such as being able to comprehend and write effective reports and design documentation, make effective presentations, and give and receive clear instructions Project Management: An ability to demonstrate knowledge and understanding of engineering and management principles and apply these to one’s own work, as a member or leader in a team, to manage projects in multidisciplinary environments Life Long Learning: An ability to recognize the need for, and have the preparation and ability to engage in independent and life-long learning in the broadest context of technological change without disturbing the balance of life
Program Learning Outcomes

Title: Research Labs & Facilities
Content:
--- Propulsion Lab ---
Propulsion is the basic concept of operating and designing of propulsion devices which include engines and power plants. In our labs, students will be able to examine motors, engines, and compressors and strive to improve their performances and reliability. They would be primarily working to design propulsion systems and parts such as propellant systems, engines, pumps, pressurization tanks, and control systems.
DSC_0103
--- Aerodynamics ---
The concept of aerodynamics is the investigation into the air flow around objects and how it affects their behavior. Our labs contain all the necessary apparatuses required to study and practically apply the laws of aerodynamics and are a valuable resource for engineering project testing, fluid mechanics coursework and alternative energy courses.
--- Control Systems ---
Control Systems are mostly utilized in the management, commandment, and regulation of devices. In our state-of-the-art labs students shall be able to conduct experiments with the concept of control system analysis and design in time and frequency domain both by classical methods and modern techniques.
1 (1)
--- Mechanics of Materials ---
Mechanics of Materials is the study of characteristics of a material subjected to stresses and strains. Our lab provides the students with basic tools to determine stress, strain and perform deformation analysis, apply methods for determining the effects produced by applying various loads on different materials where they shall be able to use their findings to design structures and components for various aeronautical devices.
--- Workshop Technology ---
Equipped with all the necessary tools required for basic knowledge of machines, welding, carpentry and fitting, our workshop technology lab is designed to let students develop the practical workshop skills by gaining basic training on converting technical drawing into practical workshop models.
1 (2)
--- Unmanned Aerial Vehicle ---
UAV stands for Unmanned Aerial Vehicle, something that can fly without a pilot onboard. We have a serviced and fully equipped UAV laboratory at IST. Students can design and fabricate any kind of UAV in the lab with ample space. Most importantly the lab provides laser cutting and 3D printing.
3 (1)
--- Modeling and Simulation Lab ---
Modeling and Simulation Lab provides high end computational facilities to students, faculty and researchers, having modeling & simulation or computational requirements at Institute of Space Technology (IST). This Project lab is established to provide software based solutions to engineering problems by using state of the art computers in a conducive environment. Modeling and Simulation Lab is the host to the IST’s computational research capability that includes high end computational resources. The lab also aims to facilitate collaborative bodies from across the nation.
1 (3)
--- Fluid Dynamics ---
Fluid Dynamics examines and describes the flow of fluids which include liquids and gases. Our labs contain flow measurement devices, flow visualization apparatus, hydraulic pumps, and turbines where students will be able to experimentally demonstrate their knowledge and familiarize themselves with the working principles by recording calculations.
--- Flight Vehicle Design ---
Flight vehicle designing relates to the designing of aerial vehicles keeping the important aerodynamics in view. Our lab at IST helps students to specify objectives and constraints and then establish scenarios. Here they can model, create intelligent agents, map strategic objectives, execute simulation and then generate surrogate models.
--- Instrumentation ---
Laboratory instruments need to have high quality and be durable in order to meet the high standards in laboratory technology. Our aerospace instrumentation laboratory carries pressure gauges, temperature controllers, automation sensors, encoders, and other high precision control and monitoring equipment.
--- Information Technology ---
Information technology is the study, design, development, implementation, support or management of computer-based information systems. We have IT labs supported with sophisticated measurements, meet technical standards, and associated testing activities. Students can perform and confront the unique technical challenges for almost all the scientifically advanced fields here.
--- Structures ---
Structures in aerodynamics and astrodynamics are designed to be lightweight, strong, and robust for them to compete against harsh conditions. In our labs, students will have the chance to design, build, and fly new aircraft concepts which shall help them to work on a variety of structural concepts that impact spacecraft, aircraft and UAS systems.
Research Labs & Facilities

Focused on the field requirements for airborne/space borne platforms, the four-year program encompasses the study of aerodynamics, propulsion, aerospace structures, flight dynamics, navigation guidance and control with application of knowledge during aerospace vehicle design.
